text.title: Strategy Memo Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding Strategy Memo Template plus the universal skip/run routing pair (referenced by the decision tree).

rule.statement: The memo MUST fit on 2 pages (≤1200 words); decks > 2 pages MUST be split or shortened.
rule.rationale: 40-slide decks bury the bet under decoration; the 2-page cap forces the author to name the bet explicitly.

rule.statement: The memo MUST contain exactly four sections: Vision, Bets, Non-Bets, Success Metrics; missing or merged sections MUST be rejected.
rule.rationale: Missing Non-Bets is the most common omission and is the section that prevents scope creep next quarter.

rule.statement: Every bet MUST be falsifiable (signal + threshold + window); aspirational bets ('be the best in category') MUST be rejected.
rule.rationale: Non-falsifiable bets cannot be re-evaluated; the falsifiability rule is the only structural gate against eternal optimism.

rule.statement: Non-Bets section MUST name ≥5 explicit non-pursuits (markets, features, channels); generic non-bets ('not enterprise') MUST be sharpened.
rule.rationale: Vague non-bets are negotiated away mid-year; named non-bets are the durable boundary.

rule.statement: Every success metric MUST list current baseline, target, owner, and review date; metrics without baseline MUST be rewritten.
rule.rationale: Baseline-less metrics make 'we improved X' unverifiable; baseline + target is the contract.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `## Applies If` do not ALL hold OR any disqualifier in `## Skip If` fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and document the skip reason in the cycle artefact.
rule.rationale: A minimum-form decision tree must route 'preconditions-not-satisfied' to skip; this rule is the canonical target.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `## Applies If` ALL hold AND no disqualifier fires, the agent MUST run the methodology end-to-end and produce the artefact specified in 02-output-contract.xml.
rule.rationale: Pair to skip-this-methodology; together they form the always-present minimal route.
